org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.view

Class AbstractViewComponent

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.arakhne.neteditor.formalism.AbstractPropertyTooler
      • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.view.AbstractViewComponent
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Serializable, Comparable<ViewComponent>, PropertyOwner, ViewComponent, ViewComponentConstants, PropertyNames
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    AbstractFigure, AbstractSubFigure

    public abstract class AbstractViewComponent
extends AbstractPropertyTooler
implements ViewComponent, ViewComponentConstants, PropertyNames
    This class is the base class for all the drawable components.
    Version:
    17.0 2013-10-23 21:03:01
    Author:
    Stéphane GALLAND, Marc BAUMGARTNER
    See Also:
    Serialized Form
    Maven Group Id:
    org.arakhne.neteditor
    Maven Artifact Id:
    neteditor-fig

    • Constructor Detail

      • AbstractViewComponent

        public AbstractViewComponent(UUID viewUUID,
                     float x,
                     float y,
                     float width,
                     float height)
        Construct a new view component.

        The specified width and height are set inconditionally. The minimal width becomes the min between the specified width and the ViewComponentConstants.DEFAULT_MINIMAL_SIZE. The minimal height becomes the min between the specified height and the ViewComponentConstants.DEFAULT_MINIMAL_SIZE. The maximal width becomes the max between the specified width and the ViewComponentConstants.DEFAULT_MAXIMAL_SIZE. The maximal height becomes the max between the specified height and the ViewComponentConstants.DEFAULT_MAXIMAL_SIZE.

        Parameters:
        viewUUID - is the UUID of the view that is enclosing this figure.
        x - horizontal position of this Fig.
        y - vertical position of this Fig.
        width - width of this Fig.
        height - height of this Fig.

    • Method Detail

      • propGetImage

        public static Image propGetImage(String name,
                 Image defaultValue,
                 boolean forceDefaultIfNull,
                 Map<String,Object> properties)
        Utility function that permits to extract a property image from a map. This function could reply null if the given map contains a property with null value. In this case the default value is ignored. The default value is replied only if the given map does not contains the name. The parameter forceDefaultIfNull overrides this behavior by forcing the use of the default value when the map contains a null.
        Parameters:
        name - is the name of the property.
        properties - is the set of properties.
        defaultValue - is the default value.
        forceDefaultIfNull - indicates if the default value must be replied when a null value is found in the map.
        Returns:
        the property value

      • propGetColor

        public static Color propGetColor(String name,
                 Color defaultValue,
                 boolean forceDefaultIfNull,
                 Map<String,Object> properties)
        Utility function that permits to extract a property color from a map. This function could reply null if the given map contains a property with null value. In this case the default value is ignored. The default value is replied only if the given map does not contains the name. The parameter forceDefaultIfNull overrides this behavior by forcing the use of the default value when the map contains a null.
        Parameters:
        name - is the name of the property.
        properties - is the set of properties.
        defaultValue - is the default value.
        forceDefaultIfNull - indicates if the default value must be replied when a null value is found in the map.
        Returns:
        the property value

      • getMetamodelSpecification

        public URL getMetamodelSpecification()
        Replies the URL that permits to download the specified of the metamodel for this element.
        Specified by:
        getMetamodelSpecification in interface ViewComponent
        Returns:
        the url of the schema for this figure. If null the default specification from NetEditor is assumed.

      • updateAssociatedGeometry

        protected abstract void updateAssociatedGeometry()
        Invoked to update the geometry of any associated object when the shape of this component has changed. This function may change the coerced figures.

      • addAssociatedFigureIntoView

        public CoercedFigure addAssociatedFigureIntoView(String figureId,
                                        CoercedFigure figure)
        Link this view component to another figure in the view. The associated figure moved according to the anchor point provided to it by this view component.
        Specified by:
        addAssociatedFigureIntoView in interface ViewComponent
        Parameters:
        figureId - is the identifier for the associated figure.
        figure - is the figure to add in the view.
        Returns:
        the coerced figure previously associated to the specified name.

      • getAssociatedFigureInView

        public CoercedFigure getAssociatedFigureInView(String figureId)
        Replies the associated figure with the specified identifier. The associated figure moved according to the anchor point provided to it by this view component.
        Specified by:
        getAssociatedFigureInView in interface ViewComponent
        Parameters:
        figureId - is the identifier for the associated figure.
        Returns:
        the associated figure with the specified id; or null.

      • getAssociatedFiguresInView

        public Map<String,CoercedFigure> getAssociatedFiguresInView()
        Replies the associated figures. The associated figure moved according to the anchor point provided to it by this view component.
        Specified by:
        getAssociatedFiguresInView in interface ViewComponent
        Returns:
        the associated figures; or null.

      • removeAssociatedFigureFromView

        public CoercedFigure removeAssociatedFigureFromView(String figureId)
        Notifies listeners about the removal of a figure from the view. The associated figure moved according to the anchor point provided to it by this view component.
        Specified by:
        removeAssociatedFigureFromView in interface ViewComponent
        Parameters:
        figureId - is the identifier for the associated figure.
        Returns:
        the removed figure; or null.

      • contains

        public boolean contains(float x,
               float y)
        Reply true if given coords are in the bounds of this Fig. This function takes into account only the bounds of the figure.
        Specified by:
        contains in interface ViewComponent
        Parameters:
        x - horizontal coord.
        y - vertical coord.
        Returns:
        true if the point (x,y) is in this Fig.

      • contains

        public boolean contains(Rectangle2f r)
        Description copied from interface: ViewComponent
        Reply true if the given rectangle is inside this component.
        Specified by:
        contains in interface ViewComponent
        Parameters:
        r - a rectangle
        Returns:
        true if the given rectangle is inside this component; otherwise false.

      • intersects

        public boolean intersects(Shape2f r)
        Reply true if the object intersects the given shape.
        Specified by:
        intersects in interface ViewComponent
        Parameters:
        r - a shape
        Returns:
        true if the figure intersects the given shape. otherwise false

      • computeBounds

        protected Rectangle2f computeBounds()
        This function compute the bounds.

        This function is not expected to be directly called by you! So, never call it. This function is called by getBounds() when the buffered damaged bounds is invalid.

        This function does not change the bounds stored in this AbstractViewComponent (that is replied by getBounds(). Its purpose is to provide an overridable implementation of the algorithm to compute the bounds. This algorithm may be specialized by the subclasses.

        Returns:
        the bounds.

      • getBounds

        public final Rectangle2f getBounds()
        Reply a copy of the position and the dimension of this Fig.

        Note that the coordinates replied by this function may be global (eg, screen coordinates) or local (eg, coordinates to the origin of a figure).

        Specified by:
        getBounds in interface ViewComponent
        Returns:
        position and dimension.
        See Also:
        computeBounds()

      • onSizeUpdated

        protected void onSizeUpdated(float oldWidth,
                 float newWidth,
                 float oldHeight,
                 float newHeight)
        Invoked each time the size (and not the position) of the component have changed. This function is declared to be overridden by subclasses to do something each time the size changed. By default this function does nothing.
        Parameters:
        oldWidth - is the old value for the width.
        newWidth - is the new value for the width.
        oldHeight - is the old value for the height.
        newHeight - is the new value for the height.
        See Also:
        onSizeUpdated(float,float,float,float), onPositionUpdated(float,float,float,float), onBoundsUpdated(float,float,float,float,float,float,float,float)

      • onPositionUpdated

        protected void onPositionUpdated(float oldX,
                     float newX,
                     float oldY,
                     float newY)
        Invoked each time the position (and not the size) of the component have changed. This function is declared to be overridden by subclasses to do something each time the position changed. By default this function does nothing.
        Parameters:
        oldX - is the old value of the X position.
        newX - is the new value of the X position.
        oldY - is the old value of the Y position.
        newY - is the new value of the Y position.
        See Also:
        onSizeUpdated(float,float,float,float), onPositionUpdated(float,float,float,float), onBoundsUpdated(float,float,float,float,float,float,float,float)

      • onBoundsUpdated

        protected void onBoundsUpdated(float oldX,
                   float newX,
                   float oldY,
                   float newY,
                   float oldWidth,
                   float newWidth,
                   float oldHeight,
                   float newHeight)
        Invoked each time the position and the size of the component have changed. This function is declared to be overridden by subclasses to do something each time the position changed. By default this function does nothing.
        Parameters:
        oldX - is the old value of the X position.
        newX - is the new value of the X position.
        oldY - is the old value of the Y position.
        newY - is the new value of the Y position.
        oldWidth - is the old value for the width.
        newWidth - is the new value for the width.
        oldHeight - is the old value for the height.
        newHeight - is the new value for the height.
        See Also:
        onSizeUpdated(float,float,float,float), onPositionUpdated(float,float,float,float), onBoundsUpdated(float,float,float,float,float,float,float,float)

      • setBounds

        public final void setBounds(Rectangle2f bounds)
        Change the position and the dimension of this Fig.
        Specified by:
        setBounds in interface ViewComponent

             float y,
             float width,
             float height)

        setBounds in interface ViewComponent
        Parameters:

        y - vertical position of this Fig.

        height - height of this Fig.

           float height)

        Specified by:
        getHeight in interface ViewComponent
        Returns:
        height.

      • getSize

        public Dimension getSize()
        Reply the size of this Fig.
        Specified by:
        getSize in interface ViewComponent
        Returns:
        the size of this Fig.

      • cleanUp

        public void cleanUp()
        Clean this Fig and all its internal temprary fields.
        Specified by:
        cleanUp in interface ViewComponent

      • getClip

        public Shape2f getClip(Rectangle2f figureBounds)
        Replies the clipping shape for this node according to the specified bounds of the figure. The specified bounds of the figure may be different of the current bounds of the figure. So that it is not recommanded to invoke ViewComponent.getBounds(), ViewComponent.getX(), ViewComponent.getY(), ViewComponent.getWidth(), and ViewComponent.getHeight().
        Specified by:
        getClip in interface ViewComponent
        Parameters:
        figureBounds - are the bounds of the figure to assume to compute the clipping object.
        Returns:
        the clipping shape, or null to use the default.

      • addViewComponentRepaintListener

        public void addViewComponentRepaintListener(ViewComponentLayoutListener listener)
        Add listener on repaint requests.
        Specified by:
        addViewComponentRepaintListener in interface ViewComponent

      • removeViewComponentRepaintListener

        public void removeViewComponentRepaintListener(ViewComponentLayoutListener listener)
        Add listener on repaint requests.
        Specified by:
        removeViewComponentRepaintListener in interface ViewComponent

      • repaint

        public void repaint(boolean boundChanged)
        Repaint the component.
        Specified by:
        repaint in interface ViewComponent
        Parameters:
        boundChanged - indicates if the repaint is due to a change in the bounds; or not.

        Reply the color of the lines.
        Specified by:

        the color of the lines.

               float y)
        Change the position of this Fig.
        Specified by:
        setLocation in interface ViewComponent
        Parameters:

        y - the new vertical position of this Fig.

             float dy)
        Move this Fig.

        translate in interface ViewComponent
        Parameters:
        dx - horizontal movement dimension.
        dy - vertical movement dimension.
